channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: GOP Rep. Elise Stefanik is considering a run for governor of New York
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rep. Elise Stefanik, a member of House Republican leadership and onetime nominee for U.S. ambassador to the United Nations, is considering a run for New York governor, according to a person close to her. The person was granted anonymity to discuss internal deliberations.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE wanted to assign staff to the nonprofit Vera Institute of Justice because it got federal funds
item.description: The nonprofit Vera Institute of Justice says staff from Elon Musk’s DOGE contacted them to “assign” a team to the organization and said DOGE staff told them they intended to “assign” teams to all nonprofits that receive funds appropriated by Congress.
item.dc:creator: Thalia Beaty, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The State Department closes the office that flags disinformation from Russia, China and Iran
item.description: The State Department has shut down the office that sought to deal with misinformation and disinformation that Russia, China and Iran have been accused of spreading.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Duffy contrasts Biden-era 'drone fiasco' with Trump admin's 'radical transparency' after FAA announces testing
item.description: Transportation Sec. Sean Duffy contrasted what he described as the Biden administration's "drone fiasco" with the Trump administration's "radical transparency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance to meet Modi, Meloni during trip to India and Italy with wife Usha
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance and his family will travel to Italy and India this week and next to meet with leaders and visit cultural sites.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP's legal threats sink Democrats' billboard attacks over Medicaid
item.description: The House GOP’s campaign arm in recent weeks has successfully pressed three advertising companies to pull down Democratic billboard displays bashing vulnerable Republicans over Medicaid — a setback to Democratic campaigners hoping to make health care a liability for battleground Republicans around t
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance gears up to talk economic priorities during trips to Italy, India
item.description: Vance and the second family are poised to meet with and “discuss shared economic and geopolitical priorities with leaders in each country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EPA chief takes on Mexican 'sewage crisis' flowing into US waters where Navy SEALs train
item.description: EPA administrator Lee Zeldin is expected to visit San Diego Tuesday to view the Tijuana sewage problem leaking into US waters and meet with Navy SEALs who train there.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance to visit leaders of Italy, India
item.description: Vice President Vance will travel next week to Italy and India to meet with leaders there and visit cultural sites. Vance will be joined by second lady Usha Vance and their children for the trip, which will begin Friday with a trip to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moderate Republicans draw red line on Medicaid cuts in Trump agenda bill
item.description: Vulnerable and moderate House Republicans are warning House GOP leaders that they will not support the “one big, beautiful bill” encompassing President Trump’s legislative agenda if it includes cuts to Medicaid benefits.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's joining tariff talks with Japan as US seeks deals amid trade wars
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's joining his economic advisers for White House negotiations over tariffs and trade with a Japanese official.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Major university medical center accused of hiding DEI programs, influential Senator calls them out
item.description: After Vanderbilt University Medical Center was allegedly caught hiding their DEI resources, Tennessee GOP Sen. Marsha Blackburn is calling on the medical school to comply with President Trump's orders demanding they such programs be halted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers press Hope Florida officials over political contributions
item.description: Lawmakers questioned Hope Florida officials Tuesday morning to further investigate those involved in the ongoing probe into the non-profit and a mysterious money trail tied to a $10 million Medicaid settlement.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Border deployment vehicle accident kills two service members, third in critical condition
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two service members deployed to the U.S. Southern border were killed and a third is in serious condition after a vehicle accident near Santa Teresa, New Mexico, the military announced late Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's lawyer says no proof of MS-13 affiliation
item.description: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's attorney, Benjamin Osorio, joined "CUOMO" for a live exclusive interview to discuss his client's status.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Sen. Bernie Sanders, Rep. AOC take on Bakersfield, push to unseat incumbent Congressman David Valadao
item.description: All of Tuesday morning into late afternoon, a crowd of more than 4,000 -- included counter protesters -- gathered in downtown Bakersfield to welcome the progressive duo.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden alleges Trump has 'taken a hatchet' to Social Security in his first post-presidency speech
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden warned on Tuesday that President Donald Trump has “taken a hatchet” to Social Security, weighing in on a critical issue for millions of Americans as the 82-year-old Democrat briefly returned to the national stage.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department can cut funding for legal guidance for people facing deportation, US judge says
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday allowed the Justice Department to temporarily stop funding legal education programs for people facing deportation or immigration court while a lawsuit brought by the organizations that provide the service moves forward in court. The decision from U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ten Commandments in classrooms 'grave concern' to Baptist pastor
item.description: A bill requiring the display of the Ten Commandments in public buildings and classrooms was signed into law in Arkansas on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro and the arson at his official residence
item.description: An independent expert will review security at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence after investigators accused a man of scaling a wall and setting fire to the mansion.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Shipkowski And Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: “Threatening to harm public officials is a criminal act that cannot be excused as political discourse,” acting U.S. Attorney Richard Moultrie, Jr. said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: 281 bills fall victim to deadline at Nevada Legislature. Here are the highlights
item.description: A total of 281 bills missed a Friday deadline to win committee passage in Carson City, the first major deadline at the Nevada Legislature this session. If that seems like a lot, consider that 875 bills are still alive.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House launches national security investigation into pharma, semiconductors
item.description: The Trump administration disclosed Monday that it had opened an investigation into the effects on national security of importing certain pharmaceuticals, a move widely seen as the prelude to initiating tariffs on prescription drugs.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: El Salvador's Bukele says he won’t return Abrego Garcia to US
item.description: President Nayib Bukele called Abrego Garcia “a terrorist” and said he had no power to return Abrego Garcia to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Tariffs could impact Tampa Bay shoppers, businesses by summer, economist says
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Cheri Hudson owns clothing store Imagine That!!! in South Tampa. It has been her business for 25 years. She is a supporter of President Trump’s trade policies. “I trust Donald Trump. It's only going to be a matter of time. The tariffs are fair.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas movie studio plan changing with new players, tech/education emphasis
item.description: A global player, MBS Group, is stepping into the plan for a $900 million movie studio at the UNLV Harry Reid Research and Technology Park, near the 215 Beltway and Durango Drive in the southwest Las Vegas valley. Birtcher Development LLC is the project's developer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: President Trump directed agencies that regulate energy and the environment to sunset a wide array of environmental protections in an executive order issued Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Legislature moving bills as Friday deadline nears; ghost kitchens, open enrollment among winners
item.description: Up against a Friday deadline for bills to be passed out of committee, Nevada lawmakers are working this week to move mountains of legislation in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five Black and Hispanic men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers cut 6 new positions in Nevada Department of Public Safety budget request
item.description: Investigation backlogs and training needs within the Nevada Department of Public Safety are among the justifications for new positions in the state budget, but lawmakers said Wednesday there's just not enough money to pay for everything.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
